title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
Term Insurance for People recovered from COVID-19
The global pandemic was a tough time for many. We either suffered the illness ourselves or saw close ones battle it. While some had it easy with their immune system strong enough to counter the attack, others even lost their lives. This sudden demise of a family member was not only emotional wrecking for the rest of them but was also financially challenging. This brought to the forefront the importance of term life insurance policies. However, for someone who recovered from Covid-19, availing of a term cover could be challenging. Here we discuss the nuances.
What has changed since the pandemic?
The insurance companies, during the pandemic, had to settle an unprecedented number of claims. The insurance companies also had to wake up to some new set of risks that they had not factored in earlier. The whirlpool of uncertainty during the pandemic not only iterated the need for term life insurance policies for us but also brought to light a new set of risks that needed to be factored in by the insurance companies.
In the year 2021, insurance companies came up with a set of systematic rules after paying out over Rs 11,0602 crore as death claims due to Covid-19. In fact, there had been more than 1.3 lakhs of covid-related death claims as of Oct 2021.This could determine the eligibility of the applicant. While a person who recovered from Covid-19 can apply for term insurance, it is essential to understand how the cover will work if the applicant is affected by the virus.
Term plan coverage for Covid-19 patients:
Ideally, term insurance benefits are highest if it is taken as early as possible. However, term insurance plans cannot be bought by an individual if he has not yet recovered from Covid 191. But, if you already hold a term insurance plan before your covid infection, any claim against the policy would be honoured. Typically, insurance companies prefer to issue term policies for individuals who have been in good health for the past 3 to 6 months. Some companies may look for a longer track record of good health.
The insurance companies were under tremendous stress as they settled numerous claims under the general death provision. Citing the situation around, many individuals drew up term insurance to ensure that their family remained financially secure if an unforeseen demise were to occur due to the deadly virus. If you also wish to apply for a term insurance plan, you can use the term insurance calculator to know your premium amount.
When to apply for term insurance?
The insurance companies have the right to categorically reject proposals of applicants who are suffering from Covid-19 at the time of applying or have only recently recovered from a severe bout of infection. It is recommended that you apply for term insurance with any competent insurer only after you have recovered completely from Covid 19 and have had no major illness over at least 6 months. The insurance companies have also tightened their underwriting norms and revised their premium rates after incorporating new risks that have emerged over the past 2 years.
The long-term impact of Coronavirus on infected individuals remains a mystery. From the insurer's perspective, this essentially means that there are a set of risks that have not been unearthed yet. Every insurance company has different underwriting norms. While some companies are staying cautious by keeping the 6 months post-recovery rule for the issuance of term policies to individuals, others have kept the cooling period at 3 months.
Additional documentation required after Covid-19:
Apart from the usual proof of identity, address and income, you need to provide your medical documents, which detail your Covid-19 diagnosis. This is pertinent as you need to prove that the cooling period has elapsed and you are eligible for the term insurance policy.
Of late, there is emphasis on the vaccination against covid-19. It has become mandatory to even visit public places and travel out of the country. Your vaccination certificate has to be maintained and produced to avail term insurance policy. Many insurers insist on additional medical tests to rule out certain medical risks arising out of previous infections.
Why is it necessary to make accurate declarations?
The insurer may seek additional information regarding your travel plans and history to ensure that you are not planning a visit to a Corona-infected nation shortly after availing of the term insurance plan. Any life insurance contract is issued under the principle of “Utmost Good Faith” or “Uberrima fides”. It is important that you provide accurate information. If you fail to do so, the insurance company may reject your application.
If any information you have provided about an existing medical condition, lifestyle choices, medical history, or documentation is incorrect/fraudulent, then your claims will be rejected. To ensure that your claims are processed seamlessly, ensure to provide accurate and detailed information.
The insurance companies are already wary about onboarding customers amidst the pandemic, more so if someone has been infected recently, then they are all the more jittery. The pandemic qualifies as a chronic disease. Hence the scrutiny for patients recovered from Covid-19 is more intricate and severe. Due to the pandemic, most people fail to qualify due to the cool-off period. This acts as another layer of risk mitigation for the insurers. In conclusion:
Both we, as individuals and insurers, are faced with a new ecology. Such challenging times help us evolve and adapt. While it may take time to adjust to the “new normal”, it is evident that there are more reasons why it is important to hedge your life risk.
